# Open peer-reviewed Raetic references

Retrieved 2026-07-10. These files are reference literature, not corpus rows and
not gold annotations. Their claims must be traced to the cited inscription and
edition before being converted into labels.

## Files

### `salomon_2020_raetic_overview.pdf`

- Corinna Salomon, “Raetic”, *Palaeohispanica* 20 (2020), pp. 263–298.
- DOI: <https://doi.org/10.36707/palaeohispanica.v0i20.380>
- Article page: <https://www.ifc-ojs.es/index.php/palaeohispanica/article/view/380>
- Download used: <https://www.ifc-ojs.es/index.php/palaeohispanica/article/download/380/358>
- 36 PDF pages; 1,905,539 bytes as retrieved.
- License: CC BY-NC-SA. The journal states that all journal content is under
  Creative Commons CC BY-NC-SA and is openly accessible.
- Utility: a compact, peer-reviewed survey of corpus structure, editions,
  chronology and distribution, inscription/support types, alphabets,
  onomastics, language, open problems, and future research directions.

### `salomon_2020_raetic_personal_names.pdf`

- Corinna Salomon, “Some remarks on the personal name system of Raetic”,
  *Namenkundliche Informationen* 112 (2020), pp. 375–408.
- DOI: <https://doi.org/10.58938/ni629>
- Article page: <https://namenkundliche-informationen.de/ni/article/view/629>
- Download used: <https://namenkundliche-informationen.de/ni/article/download/629/618>
- 34 PDF pages; 2,366,810 bytes as retrieved.
- License: CC BY 3.0. The article metadata specifically records
  <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/de/>; the article-page license
  badge links to CC BY 3.0 International.
- Quantitative utility stated by the paper: about 160 inscriptions lend
  themselves to linguistic analysis; about 70 sequences are personal names
  with some certainty, about 50 more are possible, 12 individual names are
  argued to recur, and about 45 names admit at least partial analysis or
  etymology. These are author estimates/hypotheses, not independent labels.

## Use in this project

The onomastic tables provide a reproducible target for testing name/patronym
segmentation against the local TIR snapshot. In particular, suffixes `-nu` and
`-na`, the individual-name/patronym pairs in table 4, and the author’s explicit
uncertainty notes should be represented as competing analyses rather than
collapsed into a single lexicon. The overview is the provenance source for
alphabet, genre, chronology, and corpus-selection decisions.

Integrity hashes are in `SHA256SUMS`. Redistribution must preserve attribution
and the applicable Creative Commons terms.
